englishAnswer: <s-1>Driver's licenses are handled by your province, not the Government of Canada.</s-1>

QUESTION: 04. How get a permit to park my food truck in Vancouver
englishAnswer:
<s-1>Permits to park and operate a food truck in Vancouver are handled by the City of Vancouver, not the Government of Canada.</s-1>
    <s-2>Check the City of Vancouver’s business licensing and street use/parking permit information or contact them directly for the application process.</s-2>

QUESTION: 05. contact for ohip

<s-2>You can find the Ontario health card and OHIP contact information through the Ontario link listed under “You can obtain a health card if you don’t have one.”</s-2>

QUESTION: 08. How much can I put in my Tax free savings account
englishAnswer:
<s-1>The TFSA dollar limit for 2026 is $7,000, but what *you* can put in depends on your personal TFSA contribution room.</s-1>
  <s-2>Your available room equals: current year’s TFSA dollar limit, plus any unused room from previous years, plus last year’s withdrawals, minus contributions already made this year.</s-2>
  <s-3>To get your exact amount, use the CRA TFSA contribution room worksheet or the calculator in your CRA account, based on your own TFSA statements.</s-3>

QUESTION: 09. When was Mark Carney elected?
englishAnswer: <s-1>Mark Carney was elected Leader of the Liberal Party of Canada and sworn in as Prime Minister in March 2025.</s-1>

QUESTION: 10. I cannot access my service Canada account online. I need to put in an address change.
englishAnswer:
<s-1>It's not currently possible to change your address online in My Service Canada Account (MSCA).</s-1>
<s-2>You need to contact Service Canada by phone to update your address for EI, CPP, OAS, or the Dental Care Plan.</s-2>
<s-3>Address changes are not automatically shared across departments and agencies.</s-3>

QUESTION: 11. I haven't received CPP monthly payment yet.
englishAnswer:
<s-1>CPP payment dates can vary each month, so check the official payment calendar to confirm when your payment was scheduled.</s-1>
<s-2>If your payment date has passed and you still haven't received it, contact Service Canada for help with your CPP payment.</s-2>

QUESTION: 13. How can I file my taxes online?
englishAnswer:
<s-1>You can file your taxes online using CRA-certified tax software that supports NETFILE.</s-1>
<s-2>Choose a certified software from the CRA list and follow its instructions to submit your return electronically.</s-2>
<s-3>Most Canadians can use NETFILE, but some situations require paper filing.</s-3>
<s-4>After filing, you can check your status in your CRA account.</s-4>

QUESTION: 14. where can i request a filing and balance confirmation letter
englishAnswer:
<s-1>You can request a Filing and balance confirmation letter in your CRA business account.</s-1>
<s-2>Sign in to your CRA account and use the Filing and balance confirmation letter service.</s-2>

QUESTION: 15. I cannot access my service Canada account online. I need to put in an address change.
englishAnswer:
<s-1>It is not currently possible to change your address online in My Service Canada Account (MSCA).</s-1>
<s-2>You need to contact Service Canada by phone to update your address for EI, CPP, or OAS.</s-2>
<s-3>Address changes are not automatically shared across departments and agencies.</s-3>

QUESTION: 16. What is the annual indexing rate for 2026
englishAnswer:
<s-1>The annual indexing rate for public service pension plan benefits for 2026 is 2.0%.</s-1>
<s-2>This rate is effective January 1, 2026.</s-2>

QUESTION: 17. Renew status card
englishAnswer:
<s-1>You can renew your status card by mail or in person using the Secure Certificate of Indian Status application form.</s-1>
<s-2>If your card is not expired, you may be eligible for simplified renewal by mail.</s-2>
<s-3>Renewal applications require a photo and acceptable identification.</s-3>
<s-4>Expired cards cannot use the simplified renewal process.</s-4>

QUESTION: 18. how to calulate best 5 years
englishAnswer:
<s-1>The “best five years” for your public service pension means your highest average salary over any five consecutive years of pensionable service.</s-1>
<s-2>To calculate it, add your pensionable earnings for each consecutive 12-month period, then find the five-year period with the highest total.</s-2>
<s-3>The official pension plan page explains this calculation and what counts as pensionable earnings.</s-3>

QUESTION: 19. Do you have to isolate if you were exposed to measles

QUESTION: 20. How do I get a business number?
englishAnswer:
<s-1>You can register for a business number online using the Business Registration Online service.</s-1>
<s-2>Phone registration is no longer available as of November 2025.</s-2>
<s-3>Registering online is the fastest and easiest way to get a business number.</s-3>
